mappings.rationaleAI: The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 directly established the rules for the European partition of Africa and created the Congo Free State, matching outline path D.1 on the imperialistic scramble for African colonies and the Berlin West Africa Conference.

Examines the roots of Africa's crises in European colonialism and the Berlin Conference partition, the arbitrary drawing of colonial borders, and the disruption of pre-colonial African political structures -- directly covering sub-Saharan Africa's colonial experience under paths D.1 and D.2.

The Mokrani Revolt of 1871 was a major Algerian uprising against French colonial rule, directly addressing Algeria from 1830 to 1920 under French conquest and national resistance as outlined in B.2.a.

The Russo-Japanese War (1904-05) is relevant to the Ottoman Empire's dissolution context, and its demonstration of modern industrial warfare relates to the broader geopolitical shifts affecting Southwest Asia, but its primary relevance here is tangential. Reject.
The Indian Mutiny of 1857 led to direct Crown governance, and the Ever Victorious Army's Gordon later served in Sudan -- but the episode focuses on India, not this section's geography. Reject.
The Taiping Rebellion's Ever Victorious Army under Gordon connects to the same Gordon later central to the Sudan, but the episode itself is about China. Reject.

Disraeli's Congress of Berlin (1878) reshaped the Ottoman Empire's boundaries and his purchase of Suez Canal shares directly affected Egypt's financial entanglement with European powers, relevant to paths A.2.b (crisis of 1875-78) and B.1.a (Egypt and the Suez Canal).
Annie Besant's involvement in Indian nationalism tangentially connects to broader anti-colonial movements but does not substantively address the outlined geography. Reject.
